name: Org Zhixing Demo

records.outline: Build the visible Org life surface
records.title: Build the visible Org life surface
records.bodyPreview:
The first milestone is a browser surface that can display blog notes, records,
agenda-like planning data, links, properties, and attachment evidence from the
same Org source.

records.outline: Blog / Why Org belongs in the browser
records.title: Why Org belongs in the browser
records.bodyPreview:
Rust owns the parser. TypeScript owns interaction, layout, caching, and display.
The boundary is a compact WASM DTO instead of a reimplemented parser.

records.title: A small public changelog
records.bodyPreview:
This post can be rendered as a blog card and also indexed as an ordinary user
record.

records.title: Capture parser decisions
records.bodyPreview:
Keep the source in Org. Let the frontend ask for outline, section index, lint,
attachments, and rendered HTML projections.

records.title: Sketch attachment use cases
records.bodyPreview:
Attachment metadata should be visible without filesystem access. Example:
[[attachment:architecture.png::255][architecture sketch]].

records.outline: Agenda / Parser-owned agenda workspace / Wire the agendaView worker projection
records.title: Wire the agendaView worker projection
records.bodyPreview:
Use the Rust agenda view as the source of truth for sort receipts, blocker
evidence, and visible candidate rows.

records.outline: Agenda / Parser-owned agenda workspace / Render grouped agenda cards
records.title: Render grouped agenda cards
records.bodyPreview:
This row is intentionally under an ORDERED parent so the frontend can show a
parser-owned blocker edge without recomputing Org semantics in TypeScript.

records.title: Publish the demo package
records.bodyPreview: Ship enough surface for a personal blog, records, and planning dashboard.

records.title: Review deadline posture
records.bodyPreview:
Deadline rows should become their own pressure surface instead of disappearing
inside a flat chronological list.

records.outline: Agenda / Validate the local worker protocol
records.title: Validate the local worker protocol
records.bodyPreview: The worker session can update the source and return snapshot projections.

html:
Build the visible Org life surface

The first milestone is a browser surface that can display blog notes, records, agenda-like planning data, links, properties, and attachment evidence from the same Org source.

Blog

Why Org belongs in the browser

Rust owns the parser. TypeScript owns interaction, layout, caching, and display. The boundary is a compact WASM DTO instead of a reimplemented parser.

A small public changelog

This post can be rendered as a blog card and also indexed as an ordinary user record.

Records

Capture parser decisions

Keep the source in Org. Let the frontend ask for outline, section index, lint, attachments, and rendered HTML projections.

Sketch attachment use cases

Attachment metadata should be visible without filesystem access. Example: architecture sketch.

Agenda

Parser-owned agenda workspace

Wire the agendaView worker projection

Use the Rust agenda view as the source of truth for sort receipts, blocker evidence, and visible candidate rows.

Render grouped agenda cards

This row is intentionally under an ORDERED parent so the frontend can show a parser-owned blocker edge without recomputing Org semantics in TypeScript.

Publish the demo package

Ship enough surface for a personal blog, records, and planning dashboard.

Review deadline posture

Deadline rows should become their own pressure surface instead of disappearing inside a flat chronological list.

Validate the local worker protocol

The worker session can update the source and return snapshot projections.

findings.message: task `Render grouped agenda cards` is blocked by previous open sibling `Wire the agendaView worker projection` because parent `Parser-owned agenda workspace` has local ORDERED property
